title: How archaeologists found the lost medieval megacity of Angkor
highlights: reconstructs the urban grid of a city overtaken by jungle.  ...    With funding from the National Geographic Society and European Research council, archaeologist Damian Evans and his colleaguesconducted broad LiDAR surveysof Angkor in 2012 and 2015. The team's mapping rig consisted of aLeica ALS70 HP LiDAR instrumentmounted in a pod attached to the right skid of a Eurocopter AS350 B2 helicopter alongside a 60 megapixel Leica RCD30 camera.It was as if an invisible city suddenly appeared where only overgrowth and farmland existed before.
